name: Amass
description: In-depth attack surface mapping and subdomain enumeration tool by OWASP.
fullDescription: Amass performs network mapping of attack surfaces and external asset discovery using open source intelligence gathering and active reconnaissance techniques. It integrates with 30+ data sources including Certificate Transparency logs, DNS archives, search engines, and APIs to build a comprehensive picture of an organization's internet-facing infrastructure.
features: Subdomain enumeration via 30+ passive data sources
DNS brute-forcing with custom wordlists
Certificate Transparency log scraping
Reverse DNS and reverse WHOIS lookups
Integration with Shodan, Censys, VirusTotal, AlienVault
Graph database output for visualization
REST API for programmatic access
useCases: External attack surface discovery during penetration tests
Continuous asset inventory for security teams
Subdomain takeover vulnerability assessment

name: Subfinder
description: Fast passive subdomain enumeration tool by ProjectDiscovery.
fullDescription: Subfinder is a subdomain discovery tool that focuses on passive enumeration to minimize target interaction. It uses a modular architecture with 30+ passive sources and is designed for speed and reliability in CI/CD pipelines.
features: 30+ passive data sources including CertSpotter, AlienVault, ThreatBook
Modular source architecture for easy extension
JSON, CSV, and stdout output formats
Rate limiting and concurrency control
Wildcard filtering to reduce false positives
Integration with other ProjectDiscovery tools via pipeline
useCases: Initial reconnaissance during bug bounty hunting
Asset discovery in CI/CD security pipelines
Continuous monitoring of organization subdomains

name: HTTPX
description: Fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit by ProjectDiscovery.
fullDescription: HTTPX is a fast and flexible HTTP toolkit that allows security researchers to probe web servers, extract TLS/SSL certificate metadata, detect technologies via Wappalyzer integration, and perform content discovery.
features: Probes HTTP and HTTPS with smart protocol fallback
TLS/SSL certificate metadata extraction
Wappalyzer-based technology stack fingerprinting
Response status code, title, and headers extraction
Pipeline mode for chaining with other ProjectDiscovery tools
useCases: Web server fingerprinting during reconnaissance
Filtering live domains from a list of subdomains
Technology stack identification for vulnerability assessment

name: Naabu
description: Fast port scanner by ProjectDiscovery with SYN and connect scanning.
fullDescription: Naabu is a port scanning tool written in Go that focuses on speed and accuracy. It supports both SYN scan (requires root) and connect scan, with automatic port prioritization based on common services.
features: SYN scan with raw socket and connect scan fallback
Automatic port exclusion and inclusion lists
Service detection via probe-based fingerprinting
Integration with Masscan for wide-range scanning
JSON and CSV output formats
useCases: Port scanning during external penetration tests
Service discovery for attack surface mapping
Continuous monitoring of exposed services

name: Nuclei
description: Fast vulnerability scanner based on YAML templates by ProjectDiscovery.
fullDescription: Nuclei is a fast vulnerability scanner that sends requests across targets based on customizable YAML templates. With thousands of community-contributed templates covering CVEs, misconfigurations, exposed panels, and default credentials.
features: Thousands of community-contributed threat-actuated templates
Protocol support: HTTP, DNS, TCP, SSL, WHOIS, and more
Workflow engine for multi-step vulnerability chaining
Dynamic extractors and matchers for response analysis
Integration with all ProjectDiscovery tools via pipeline
useCases: Automated vulnerability scanning in CI/CD pipelines
Pre-production misconfiguration detection
Bug bounty reconnaissance automation

name: Masscan
description: High-performance TCP port scanner capable of scanning the entire internet.
fullDescription: Masscan is the fastest internet-scale port scanner, capable of transmitting 10 million packets per second using a custom TCP/IP stack for maximum throughput.
features: Capable of scanning the entire IPv4 internet in under 5 minutes
Custom TCP/IP stack for maximum throughput
Banner grabbing and service fingerprinting
Output compatible with nmap (-oX, -oB formats)
Random IP randomization to avoid rate limiting
useCases: Internet-wide asset discovery
Large-scale network reconnaissance
Identifying exposed services across cloud IP ranges

name: Nmap
description: The industry-standard network discovery and security scanning tool.
fullDescription: Nmap (Network Mapper) is the de facto standard for network discovery and security auditing. Uses raw IP packets to determine hosts, services, operating systems, and firewall rules with the Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE).
features: Multiple scan types: SYN, TCP connect, UDP, SCTP, and Idle scan
OS detection via TCP/IP stack fingerprinting
Service and version detection on open ports
Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE) with 600+ scripts
Firewall and IDS/IPS evasion techniques
useCases: Network inventory and asset management
Vulnerability scanning via NSE scripts
Firewall rule auditing and penetration testing

name: Shodan
description: Search engine for internet-connected devices and their exposed services.
fullDescription: Shodan is the first search engine for internet-connected devices, continuously scanning the entire IPv4 address space and indexing service banners from web servers, cameras, ICS, and databases.
features: Full-text search across 5+ billion indexed services
Filter by port, service, country, city, organization
Vulnerability filtering (CVE-linked devices)
REST API for programmatic access
Real-time streaming feed of new devices
useCases: Attack surface discovery for organizations
Monitoring exposed industrial control systems
Research on vulnerable internet-connected devices

name: Censys
description: Internet-wide scanning platform providing asset discovery and certificate transparency.
fullDescription: Censys continuously scans the entire public IPv4 address space and maintains a comprehensive database of TLS certificates, open ports, and service banners for asset discovery and attack surface monitoring.
features: Full IPv4 internet scan results searchable in real-time
TLS certificate transparency database
Organization asset discovery and tracking
REST and Python API access
Historical data for trend analysis
useCases: Organization-owned asset discovery
Certificate misconfiguration and expiration monitoring
Threat research and attack surface analysis

name: ZGrab
description: Fast application-layer scanner for internet-wide surveys by ZMap team.
fullDescription: ZGrab performs application-layer handshakes and banner grabs on open ports identified by ZMap, supporting multiple protocols including HTTP, HTTPS (TLS), SSH, FTP, SMTP, IMAP, POP3, and Modbus.
features: Application-layer handshake for 10+ protocols
TLS/SSL certificate chain collection
HTTP GET request and response capture
Modular scanner architecture for custom protocols
JSON-line output for large-scale processing
useCases: Internet-wide protocol-specific surveys
TLS certificate ecosystem research
Large-scale security measurement studies

name: Metasploit Framework
description: The most widely used penetration testing and exploitation framework.
fullDescription: The most widely used exploitation framework providing a complete environment for developing, testing, and executing exploit code against remote targets with thousands of exploits, payloads, and auxiliary modules.
features: 5000+ exploit modules covering all major platforms
2000+ payload module variants (staged, stageless, Meterpreter)
600+ auxiliary modules (scanners, fuzzers, sniffers)
Meterpreter advanced in-memory payload system
MSFVenom for custom payload generation
useCases: Full-scope penetration testing engagements
Vulnerability validation and proof-of-concept development
Red team operations and adversary simulation

name: Empire
description: Post-exploitation framework using PowerShell and Python agents.
fullDescription: Empire is a pure PowerShell post-exploitation agent framework with cryptologically-secure communications, supporting PowerShell agents without needing powershell.exe and rapid post-exploitation modules.
features: PowerShell agents without powershell.exe requirement
Python agents for cross-platform coverage
Multiple listener types: HTTP, HTTPS, DNS, SMB, and more
Encrypted C2 communication channels
Integrated Mimikatz for credential harvesting
Active Directory enumeration and attack modules
useCases: Internal assessment and post-exploitation
Active Directory security assessment
Red team operations with staged deployment

name: Covenant
description: ASP.NET Core-based command and control framework for red teaming.
fullDescription: Covenant is a .NET-based C2 framework with a web-based UI for managing listeners, launchers, and compromised hosts called Grunts, supporting dynamic C# compilation and in-memory execution.
features: Web-based C2 management interface
Dynamic C# code compilation and execution
Multiple listener types: HTTP, HTTPS, Bridge
Grunts (agents) with modular tasking capability
In-memory execution without writing to disk
Encrypted communication with configurable delay/jitter
useCases: Red team command and control infrastructure
Internal penetration testing with persistent access
.NET environment post-exploitation

name: Sliver
description: Open source adversary emulation and C2 framework by BishopFox.
fullDescription: Sliver is an open source C2 framework for adversary emulation and red team operations with mutually authenticated TLS-protected communications and multiple implant types.
features: Mutual TLS (mTLS) for implant-server communication
Multiple implant formats: shared library, shellcode, executable
DNS, HTTP(S), and WireGuard C2 protocols
Screenshot, keylogging, and file system operations
Process injection and migration capabilities
Armory for community-shared extensions and profiles
useCases: Adversary emulation exercises
Red team infrastructure deployment
Post-exploitation assessment and lateral movement

name: Cobalt Strike
description: Commercial adversary simulation and threat emulation software.
fullDescription: Commercial adversary simulation platform providing Beacon payloads, Malleable C2 profiles, collaborative team server, and advanced post-exploitation capabilities for professional red teams.
features: Beacon payload with HTTP/HTTPS, DNS, and SMB C2 channels
Malleable C2 profiles for traffic customization and evasion
Collaborative team server with multiple operators
Advanced lateral movement and pivoting techniques
Spear phishing campaign automation
useCases: Professional red team engagements
Adversary simulation and emulation exercises
Full-scope penetration testing with C2 requirements

name: BloodHound
description: Active Directory attack path analysis and visualization tool.
fullDescription: BloodHound uses graph theory to reveal hidden relationships in Active Directory, identifying complex attack paths that chain multiple privileges to reach domain admin.
features: Graph-based visualization of Active Directory relationships
Automatic identification of attack paths to high-value targets
SharpHound collector for data gathering
Cypher query language for custom path analysis
Session collection and analysis for lateral movement
GPO and ACL abuse path identification
useCases: Active Directory security posture assessment
Privilege escalation path identification
Hardening Active Directory deployments

name: Wazuh
description: Open source security monitoring and endpoint detection platform.
fullDescription: Wazuh is a unified XDR and SIEM platform providing security monitoring, threat detection, incident response, and compliance management across on-premises, cloud, and containerized environments.
features: File integrity monitoring (FIM) for critical file changes
Log analysis and correlation with 2000+ built-in rules
Malware detection via hash and filename monitoring
Vulnerability detection from CVE databases
Container and Kubernetes security monitoring
GDPR, PCI DSS, HIPAA compliance reporting
useCases: Enterprise-wide SIEM and security monitoring
Regulatory compliance automation and reporting
Cloud workload protection across multi-cloud environments

name: Osquery
description: SQL-based operating system instrumentation and monitoring framework.
fullDescription: Osquery exposes an operating system as a high-performance relational database, allowing SQL queries to explore system state including processes, kernel modules, network connections, file hashes, and browser plugins.
features: SQL interface to operating system state (200+ tables)
Cross-platform support: Linux, macOS, Windows, and FreeBSD
Osqueryi interactive shell for ad-hoc investigation
Osqueryd daemon for scheduled query execution
Distributed querying across fleet of endpoints
YARA integration for file scanning
useCases: Incident response and live forensic analysis
Continuous security monitoring across device fleets
Threat hunting via scheduled queries and anomaly detection

name: Velociraptor
description: Advanced endpoint monitoring, forensics, and incident response platform.
fullDescription: Velociraptor is a DFIR platform that collects, queries, and monitors endpoint state at scale using Velocidex Query Language (VQL) for flexible data collection across thousands of endpoints.
features: Velocidex Query Language (VQL) for flexible data collection
Distributed hunting across thousands of endpoints
Real-time event monitoring with server-side alerting
Automated collection using pre-built artifacts
Offline collection for air-gapped environments
Built-in notebook for collaborative analysis
useCases: Enterprise-scale incident response and forensic collection
Proactive threat hunting across endpoint fleet
Compromise assessment and breach investigation

name: LimaCharlie
description: Security infrastructure cloud providing EDR, detection, and response capabilities.
fullDescription: LimaCharlie is a cloud-based detection and response platform providing EDR sensors, network detection, file analysis, and automated response through a flexible rules engine.
features: EDR sensor with kernel-level telemetry collection
Network detection and monitoring capabilities
YARA and Sigma rule execution at the endpoint
Automated response via detection rules and playbooks
File analysis sandbox integration
REST API for full programmatic control
useCases: Small to medium business EDR deployment
Detection engineering and custom rule authoring
Automated incident response and remediation

name: SentinelOne
description: Autonomous endpoint protection with AI-driven threat detection.
fullDescription: SentinelOne provides autonomous endpoint protection through AI-driven behavioral analysis, static AI, and cloud-based threat intelligence via the Singularity XDR platform.
features: AI-powered behavioral threat detection and prevention
Autonomous response with rollback capability
Ransomware protection with file system monitoring
Cloud workload protection (CWPP) capabilities
IoT device security monitoring
useCases: Enterprise endpoint protection and EDR
Ransomware prevention and automated recovery
Multi-platform endpoint management

name: CrowdStrike Falcon
description: Cloud-native endpoint protection with threat intelligence integration.
fullDescription: CrowdStrike Falcon is a cloud-delivered endpoint protection platform correlating 5 trillion endpoint events per week with cloud-based threat intelligence for real-time visibility and protection.
features: Lightweight single-agent architecture (IOA/IOC)
Real-time indicators of attack (IOA) detection
Threat intelligence powered by CrowdStrike Falcon OverWatch
Automated containment and response workflows
Identity protection and cloud workload security
useCases: Enterprise endpoint detection and response (EDR)
Managed threat hunting via OverWatch service
Incident response readiness and breach investigation

name: Volatility
description: Advanced memory forensics framework for incident response and malware analysis.
fullDescription: The world's most widely used memory forensics framework for analyzing RAM dumps, supporting Windows, Linux, and macOS memory images with a plugin-based architecture.
features: Process and DLL enumeration from memory dumps
Network connection and socket extraction
Registry hive extraction from Windows memory
Kernel object and driver scanning
Malware detection via code injection and hook analysis
File carving from memory pages
useCases: Memory dump analysis during incident response
Rootkit and kernel-level malware detection
Advanced persistent threat (APT) investigation

name: Autopsy
description: Graphical digital forensics platform with timeline and keyword search capabilities.
fullDescription: Autopsy is the graphical interface to The Sleuth Kit providing disk analysis, file recovery, timeline creation, keyword searching, and hash set filtering with a modular ingest framework.
features: Graphical case management and evidence tree
Multi-user collaboration via centralized repository
Keyword and regex search across all files
File system timeline and event reconstruction
Hash set filtering (NSRL, custom, known bad)
Extensible ingest pipeline with Python module support
useCases: Computer forensic examination for law enforcement
Corporate internal investigations and e-discovery
Data recovery and file system analysis

name: The Sleuth Kit
description: Library and command-line tool collection for disk and file system analysis.
fullDescription: The Sleuth Kit (TSK) is a collection of command-line tools for forensic analysis of disk images and file systems, providing low-level access to file system structures for in-depth analysis.
features: File system layer analysis: NTFS, FAT, ext2/3/4, HFS+, APFS
Deleted file recovery and file carving
MFT (Master File Table) parsing for NTFS analysis
Timeline generation and body file creation
Hash database comparison (MD5, SHA-1)
useCases: Disk forensic analysis and evidence collection
Data recovery from damaged or deleted file systems
Timeline reconstruction for incident response

name: Plaso (log2timeline)
description: Super timeline and event extraction engine for digital forensics.
fullDescription: Plaso (formerly log2timeline) is a super timeline tool that extracts timestamps from 500+ artifact types and presents them chronologically for digital forensic investigations.
features: 500+ parser plugins for timeline extraction
Multiple input sources: disk images, directories, individual files
Storage backends: SQLite, Elasticsearch, and JSON
Multi-process architecture for high-throughput processing
Filtering and tagging for artifact prioritization
useCases: Super timeline creation for forensic investigations
Event reconstruction and activity timeline analysis
Multi-artifact correlation across disparate data sources

name: Rekall
description: Advanced memory forensics and analysis framework with live analysis capabilities.
fullDescription: Rekall is a memory forensics framework forked from Volatility with unique capabilities including AFF4 memory image format, live memory analysis, and integrated YARA scanning.
features: AFF4 memory image format for high-compression storage
Rekall Memory Profiler for automatic profile generation
Live memory acquisition without loading kernel drivers
Integrated YARA scanning for in-memory malware detection
Time-aware analysis plugin support
useCases: Live memory analysis on running systems
Malware analysis and rootkit detection
Forensic analysis of Linux, Windows, and macOS memory

name: DFIR-IRIS
description: Open source incident response platform for case management and collaboration.
fullDescription: DFIR-IRIS is an open source incident response platform providing case management, alert triage, evidence management, and reporting with integration into existing SOC tools via an extensive API.
features: Case management with customizable workflows
Alert ingestion from SIEM, EDR, and email sources
Evidence management with hash verification
Task assignment and tracking for incident handlers
IOC extraction and management
Report generation with customizable templates
useCases: SOC incident tracking and case management
Computer security incident response (CSIRT) operations
Evidence collection and chain of custody management

name: theHarvester
description: E-mail, subdomain, and name gathering tool for OSINT reconnaissance.
fullDescription: theHarvester gathers emails, subdomains, IPs, and URLs from public search engines, PGP key servers, social media, and other sources for OSINT reconnaissance against target organizations.
features: Email address harvesting from Google, Bing, Yahoo, and Yandex
Subdomain discovery via DNS enumeration and search engines
PGP key server searches for corporate email discovery
LinkedIn user search for employee enumeration
Shodan integration for exposed service discovery
useCases: OSINT reconnaissance for penetration testing
Email and employee enumeration for phishing assessments
Organization digital footprint analysis

name: Recon-ng
description: Full-featured web reconnaissance framework with modular architecture.
fullDescription: Recon-ng is a full-featured reconnaissance framework with a Metasploit-like interface, workspace management, 130+ modules, and built-in reporting for structured OSINT investigations.
features: Metasploit-like console interface with tab completion
Workspace management for organizing targets and data
130+ modules organized by reconnaissance category
Built-in API key management for commercial data sources
Reporting engine for HTML, CSV, and database exports
useCases: Structured OSINT reconnaissance for penetration tests
Organization digital footprint analysis
Security research and due diligence investigations

name: Maltego
description: Link analysis and data mining platform for OSINT investigations.
fullDescription: Maltego is an OSINT visualization and link analysis platform using transform-based queries to gather information and present entity relationships as interactive graphs for investigations.
features: Graph-based visualization of entity relationships
Transform engine for automated data gathering from 100+ sources
Entity types: persons, domains, IPs, emails, documents, etc.
Collaborative investigation with shared workspaces
CaseFile mode for manual evidence link analysis
useCases: Complex OSINT investigations and link analysis
Social network mapping and relationship discovery
Fraud investigation and corporate due diligence

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automated OSINT reconnaissance and vulnerability correlation tool.
fullDescription: SpiderFoot automates OSINT reconnaissance with 200+ data sources for automated footprinting, intelligence gathering, and vulnerability correlation via CLI, web UI, or REST API.
features: 200+ data sources integrated for intelligence gathering
IP, domain, email, and human name correlation
Vulnerability database correlation with discovered assets
Web-based UI with scan visualization dashboard
REST API for integration into security pipelines
useCases: Automated reconnaissance and intelligence gathering
Attack surface analysis and vulnerability correlation
Continuous monitoring of organization digital footprint

name: Maigret
description: Social media account search and username enumeration tool.
fullDescription: Maigret checks a username across 4000+ websites and social networks, collecting profile information and generating comprehensive reports for OSINT investigations.
features: Username search across 4000+ websites and social networks
Profile data extraction: name, bio, avatar, location, followers
Automatic tag extraction (personal, dating, dev, etc.)
Graphical and HTML report generation
Proxy/Tor support for anonymous searching
useCases: Person of interest OSINT investigation
Social media presence mapping for threat actors
Account verification and identity verification

name: Social Analyzer
description: Social media profile analysis and username search across 1000+ platforms.
fullDescription: Social Analyzer detects social media profiles across 1000+ platforms using a multilayered detection engine combining HTTP requests, pattern matching, and API analysis with configurable confidence scoring.
features: Profile detection across 1000+ social media platforms
Multilayer detection engine with confidence scoring
REST API for integration into analysis pipelines
CLI, web, and Docker deployment options
Risk level configuration for detection sensitivity
useCases: OSINT username investigation across multiple platforms
Social engineering attack surface assessment
Brand impersonation monitoring and detection

name: Wireshark
description: Industry-standard network protocol analyzer and packet capture tool.
fullDescription: Wireshark is the world's foremost network protocol analyzer with deep inspection of 3000+ protocols, live capture, display filters, and rich visualization for network troubleshooting and security analysis.
features: Live packet capture from any network interface
Deep inspection of 3000+ network protocols
Rich display filter language (Wireshark Filter)
Color-coding for quick protocol identification
VoIP analysis with call flow visualization
Export objects (HTTP files, SMB files, etc.)
useCases: Network troubleshooting and performance analysis
Security incident investigation and packet forensics
Protocol development and reverse engineering

name: TCPDump
description: Command-line packet analyzer for network traffic capture and inspection.
fullDescription: TCPDump is a powerful command-line packet analyzer using the libpcap library and Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) syntax for network traffic capture, filtering, and inspection.
features: Live network packet capture to file or stdout
Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) syntax for traffic filtering
Read from and write to pcap/pcapng file formats
Protocol decode for major protocols (TCP, UDP, ICMP, DNS, HTTP)
useCases: Quick network troubleshooting on headless servers
Scripted packet capture and analysis
Network security monitoring and traffic analysis

name: Zeek
description: Network security monitoring and traffic analysis framework (formerly Bro).
fullDescription: Zeek is a powerful network security monitoring tool focused on creating high-fidelity network transaction logs rather than real-time alerting, with a flexible scripting language for custom detection.
features: Network protocol analysis for 50+ protocols
High-fidelity connection, DNS, HTTP, SSL, and SMTP logs
Zeek scripting language for custom detection logic
File extraction from network streams for malware analysis
Cluster support for high-throughput environments
useCases: Enterprise network security monitoring
Network traffic forensics and incident reconstruction
Custom detection rule development via Zeek scripts

name: Suricata
description: High-performance IDS/IPS and network security monitoring engine.
fullDescription: Suricata is a high-performance Network IDS, IPS, and NSM engine by OISF supporting multi-threaded packet processing, GPU acceleration, and Lua scripting for custom detection logic.
features: Multi-threaded architecture for 10+ Gbps throughput
IDS/IPS inline mode for active traffic blocking
Automatic protocol detection and parsing (100+ protocols)
Lua scripting for custom detection logic
File extraction, MD5/SHA1/SHA256 hashing
GPU acceleration for pattern matching (CUDA)
useCases: High-throughput network intrusion detection
Inline intrusion prevention at network perimeter
Network security monitoring and threat hunting

name: Snort
description: The de facto standard open source intrusion detection and prevention system.
fullDescription: Snort is the de facto standard open source IDS/IPS by Cisco, performing real-time traffic analysis and packet logging using protocol analysis, pattern matching, and anomaly detection.
features: Real-time traffic analysis and packet logging
Protocol analysis for TCP, UDP, ICMP, and application-layer
Signature-based detection with community and registered rule sets
Inline IPS mode for active traffic blocking
Snort 3 with multi-threading and improved performance
useCases: Network perimeter intrusion detection

Test and training environments for detection engineering

name: ntopng
description: Web-based network traffic monitoring and flow analysis platform.
fullDescription: ntopng is a web-based network traffic monitoring tool providing real-time traffic visibility with NetFlow, sFlow, IPFIX collection, per-host metrics, and historical reporting.
features: Real-time network traffic visualization via web UI
NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX flow data collection and analysis

name: Sigma
description: Generic signature format for SIEM detection rules across platforms.
fullDescription: Sigma is a generic signature format for security detection rules that converts to 20+ SIEM query languages (Splunk, Elastic, QRadar, Sentinel), with 2000+ community-contributed rules.
features: Platform-agnostic detection rule format (YAML)
Automatic conversion to 20+ SIEM query languages
2000+ community-contributed detection rules
Rule categorization by MITRE ATT&CK technique
Modular rule structure with severity and confidence levels
useCases: Cross-platform detection rule development and sharing

name: YARA
description: Pattern-matching tool for malware identification and classification.
fullDescription: YARA helps malware researchers identify and classify malware samples using textual or binary pattern descriptions, with support for hexadecimal patterns, regex, and PE/ELF/Mach-O analysis modules.
features: Binary and text pattern matching with wildcard support
Hexadecimal pattern matching with jumps and offsets
Regular expression matching within files
PE/ELF/Mach-O module for structured file analysis
Math module for entropy and statistical calculations
YARA-X (next-gen) with improved performance
useCases: Malware family identification and classification
Threat hunting across file shares and endpoints
Memory forensics and malware detection via Volatility/YARA

name: Elastic Detection Rules
description: Open detection rules for Elastic Security SIEM and endpoint protection.
fullDescription: Elastic Detection Rules is the official repository of open detection rules for Elastic Security with MITRE ATT&CK mapping, risk scoring, and automated testing for production SIEM deployments.
features: Hundreds of production-ready detection rules
MITRE ATT&CK framework mapping for every rule
Custom query, EQL, and threshold rule types

useCases: Deploying SIEM detection rules in Elastic Security

description: AWS exploitation framework for penetration testing cloud environments.

features: 50+ AWS-specific penetration testing modules
IAM privilege escalation path detection
Lambda persistence and backdoor deployment
S3 data exfiltration and bucket policy analysis
CloudTrail logging detection and evasion

description: Multi-cloud security audit tool for AWS, Azure, and GCP environments.
fullDescription: ScoutSuite is an open source multi-cloud security auditing tool for AWS, Azure, GCP, and Alibaba Cloud, generating comprehensive HTML reports with categorized findings and actionable recommendations.

name: CloudFox
description: Cloud security tool for finding exploitable attack paths in AWS.

name: Ghidra
description: Software reverse engineering framework by the NSA with decompiler and analysis engine.

C-like decompiler for high-level code reconstruction
Scripting via Python, Java, and JavaScript
Multi-user project collaboration server

name: IDA Free
description: Free version of the Interactive Disassembler by Hex-Rays.
fullDescription: IDA Free provides professional-quality disassembly with cross-references, graph views, and navigation features for x86/x64 binaries as the free entry point to the industry-standard disassembler.

Named location and comment system
Cross-references (xrefs) tracking
Binary file format support: PE, ELF, Mach-O

name: Radare2
description: Advanced open source reverse engineering and binary analysis framework.
fullDescription: Radare2 is a complete reverse engineering framework supporting 180+ CPU architectures with a command-line interface, ESIL emulation, integrated debugger, and extensive scripting via r2pipe.

Command-line interface with extensive scripting (r2pipe)

name: Cutter
description: Graphical reverse engineering platform powered by Radare2.
fullDescription: Cutter is a Qt-based graphical reverse engineering platform combining Rizin (Radare2 fork) with decompilers (Ghidra, R2Dec, Snowman), debugger support, and a Python plugin system.

Graph view, disassembly view, and hex view side by side

name: x64dbg
description: Open source x64/x32 debugger for Windows with scriptable interface.
fullDescription: x64dbg is an open source Windows user-mode debugger for malware analysis and reverse engineering with an intuitive GUI, scriptable automation, and Python/JavaScript plugin support.
features: 64-bit and 32-bit Windows debugging support
Intuitive GUI with customizable layout
Memory map, register, stack, and hex dump views
Disassembler with integrated assembler (XEDParse/Keystone)
Scriptable via built-in x64dbg scripting language
Plugin system for Python, JavaScript, and C++ extensions
useCases: Windows malware analysis and behavior monitoring
Software exploitation and exploit development
Patch analysis and vulnerability research for Windows binaries

name: ImHex
description: Modern hex editor with pattern language, data visualization, and disassembly.
fullDescription: ImHex is a modern hex editor for reverse engineers with a powerful pattern language for binary structures, built-in disassembler, YARA integration, and data visualization tools.
features: Pattern language for structured binary data description
Built-in disassembler supporting x86, ARM, and more
Byte patch visualization and comparison view
Data inspector with 20+ value interpretation formats
YARA rule integration for file scanning
useCases: Binary file format analysis and reverse engineering
Malware binary dissection and string analysis
Data recovery and custom file format research
